uk: Свв. трьох отроків: Ананії, Азарії й Мисаїла.
saints.en: St. Prophet Daniel. Sts. Three Youths: Ananias, Azarias & Misael

rubrics:
Вечірня: катизма чергова. На «Господи, взиваю» 6 стихир: 3 прор. і 3 отроків, Сл.: прор., І нині: богород. за гласом «Слава». Прокімен дня. Стиховня Октоїха. По «Отче наш»: тропар «Великі подвиги віри» Сл., І нині: богород. відпустовий за гласом тропаря і за днем тижня.
Утреня: по «Бог Господь»: тропар «Великі подвиги віри» (2), Сл., І нині: богород. відпустовий за гласом тропаря прор. Катизми чергові, сідальні Октоїха. Канон Октоїха та прор. По 9-ій пісні – світильний Октоїха, Сл.: світил. прор., І нині: богородичний. Стиховня Октоїха. По «Отче наш»: тропар прор., Сл., І нині: богород. за гласом тропаря прор. і за днем тижня.
Часи: на 1-му – тропар дня; на 6-му – храму; на 3-му та 9-му – прор.
Літургія: вся Служба – дня. Ап. – Євр. 326 зач.; 10, 35-11,7. Єв. – Мр. 39 зач.; 9, 10-16.

troparion_en.text: Great are the accomplishments of faith, / for the Three Holy Youths rejoiced in the source of the flame, as if by restful waters,1 / and the Prophet Daniel appeared as a shepherd of lions as of sheep. / By their prayers, O Christ God, save our souls.

kontakion_en.text:
(The Original Melody)
You would not worship an image made by hands, O thrice-blessed youths;  /  but shielded by the ineffable Essence, you were glorified through your ordeal by fire.  /  In the midst of the unbearable fire you called upon God, crying: /  "Hasten, O compassionate One, /  and in Your mercy come to our aid,  / for if You will, You can do so."2

matched_oca_saint: The Three Holy Youths: Ananias, Azarias and Misael

troparion.text: Great are the accomplishments of faith, / for the Three Holy Youths rejoiced in the source of the flame, as if by restful waters,1 / and the Prophet Daniel appeared as a shepherd of lions as of sheep. / By their prayers, O Christ God, save our souls.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “Today the Virgin...”)
Cleansed by the Spirit  /  your pure heart became a vessel of clearest prophecy;  /  you saw things far away as if they were near.  When cast into the den you tamed the lions. / Therefore we honor you, O blessed Prophet, glorious Daniel.

oca_saints.name: The Three Holy Youths: Ananias, Azarias and Misael

troparion.text: Great are the accomplishments of faith, / for the Three Holy Youths rejoiced in the source of the flame, as if by restful waters,1 / and the Prophet Daniel appeared as a shepherd of lions as of sheep. / By their prayers, O Christ God, save our souls.

kontakion.text:
(The Original Melody)
You would not worship an image made by hands, O thrice-blessed youths;  /  but shielded by the ineffable Essence, you were glorified through your ordeal by fire.  /  In the midst of the unbearable fire you called upon God, crying: /  "Hasten, O compassionate One, /  and in Your mercy come to our aid,  / for if You will, You can do so."2

oca_saints.name: Venerable Daniel the Confessor (in Schema Stephen) of Spain, and Egypt

troparion.text: Champion of Orthodoxy, teacher of purity and of true worship, / the enlightener of the universe and the adornment of hierarchs: / all-wise father Daniel, your teachings have gleamed with light upon all things. / Intercede before Christ God to save our souls.

(Podoben: “When the stone had been sealed...”)
Let all the faithful honor with one accord Dionysios,  / the offspring of Zákynthos, the Bishop of Aegina, the guardian of the Holy Monastery of Strophada, /  crying out to him sincerely, /  “By your prayers, save those who celebrate your memory, crying to you: /  ‘Glory to Christ, Who glorified you.  / Glory to Him Who made you wondrous.  / Glory to Him Who has granted you to us as an unsleeping intercessor.’”

oca_saints.name: Martyr Avakum the Deacon of Serbia

troparion.text: As true soldiers of Christ, / you shone forth with meekness and humility / and for Christ you suffered courageously, / O Holy Martyrs Paisius and Avakum, / but your deaths proclaim to all / that it is better to die for Christ and for one’s country / than without Christ to gain the whole world.

kontakion.text: In this world you lived like angels / and by your lives fulfilled the Gospel. / You laid down your souls for faith and country, / in death you showed yourselves to be stronger than your tormentors, / therefore we celebrate your memory, / O holy venerable martyrs Paisius and Avakum.

troparion.text: As true soldiers of Christ, / you shone forth with meekness and humility / and for Christ you suffered courageously, / O Holy Martyrs Paisius and Avakum, / but your deaths proclaim to all / that it is better to die for Christ and for one’s country / than without Christ to gain the whole world.
